setup: B_Alpaca knife-edge, kill side: 205 Lv 6.0 Lancers (no hero) vs Alpaca's AURA'D Gatot-led FC1 T1 Infantry - the count one above the threshold; the Infantry dies at turn 575. Pairs with the 204v1 battle (capped at 1500).

assumptions: Gatot S2 King's Bestowal triggers == battle rounds (Stage-4 verified)

troop_passives_active.reason: Lancers are ordinary/FC<3 - not unlocked; battle stays Type 1

stats_capture.notes: Infantry panels AURA'D: 514.1 = 176.2 baseline + 337.9 ~= Gatot Expedition +337.85 (hero-screen-consistent, no alliance delta)

hero_skills.name: King's Bestowal
hero_skills.effect: fires once per attack; shield with protection = Attack x 12% for 1 turn (L2)

hero_skills.name: Royal Legion
hero_skills.effect: instills fear: -15% enemy Attack, once at battle start (L3)

notes: B_ALPACA KNIFE-EDGE PAIR (with 204v1_..._004328): 204 Lancers CANNOT kill the aura'd Gatot Infantry within the 1500-turn cap; 205 kill it at turn 575. The one-Lancer margin at N~205 is the LINEAR-pooling signature (a sqrt(N) budget would not move the cap boundary at +0.5% N).

B_Alpaca solve (K(Lan->Inf)=91 convention, FC1-base A=10 L=11): r/Lancer = 27.91x22.583/91 = 6.93 pool-units/turn; net(205) = pool/575 = 0.530 => B_Alpaca ~ 1419; K=83.7 branch => ~1543. Either way ~5.5-6x ABOVE the 257 predicted by the (A*L x Expedition%) composite - that composite law is REFUTED; B's cross-copy scaling remains open (sheets scale only x1.12-1.19 Alpaca/Mueller while B scales ~x7).
Kill-time self-consistency: with B just under 205r, net(205)=pool/575 exactly reproduces the observed 575 - strong confirmation of the subtractive linear budget MECHANISM.
Battle-report header's date digit was low-legibility in the capture; recorded as 2026-07-19 (fought minutes before the paired 00:43:28 battle; Martin presented both together).
kills cross-check: attacker kills 1 == defender losses+injured 1; defender kills 2 == attacker losses+injured 2 (lightly-injured excluded per convention).

missing: attacker FC badge (ordinary vs FC1; base identical by convention, both proc-free)
